metadata.title: Make It Move
metadata.audience: Humanitarians AI Fellows learning to use the Brutalist pipeline

beats.narration_text: Make It Move. Rebuild the idea as motion — never screenshot. Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: I have a figure from my paper. Should I screenshot it or rebuild it? How does Brutalist handle static figures?
output: Never screenshot — always rebuild as motion
ASK → RESULT: every visual has a traceable prompt

beats.narration_text: Left: the static source figure — grey, crosshatched, a screenshot from your paper. Right: the native animated bar chart — terracotta, spring-animated, native Remotion. The label in the center: REBUILT. Below: the ASK to RESULT receipt showing the Claude prompt that generated the scene. Rebuild the idea as motion. Never screenshot.

props.sparkLine: Rebuild the idea as motion. Never screenshot.

beats.narration_text: The tool choice depends on the content. Manim handles mathematical equations, geometric proofs, and physics simulations. Remotion handles bar charts, timelines, flow diagrams, and text-heavy layouts. When Claude can't decide, it generates a request card explaining exactly what it needs from you.

artifactLines: Manim: equations, geometry, physics, LaTeX-heavy content.
Remotion: bar charts, timelines, flow diagrams, text layouts.
Both are automatically chosen per beat by the pipeline.
If Claude needs input: it leaves a REQUEST CARD, not a blank.

beats.narration_text: The ASK to RESULT law: before every generated visual, there must be a traceable Claude prompt that could produce it. This keeps the pipeline auditable. If a visual doesn't make sense, you can read the original prompt and understand exactly what Claude was asked to build.

props.title: ASK→RESULT law
props.code:
# Before every Manim or Remotion beat:
# Claude logs the prompt that generated it:
#
# ASK: "build a bar chart showing precision
#       improvement from 0.72 to 0.85 across
#       5 feature configurations"
#
# RESULT: media/B02.mp4 (terracotta bars)

beats.narration_text: Rebuild the idea as motion. Never screenshot. Every visual has a traceable prompt in the ASK to RESULT log.

artifactLines: Never screenshot a figure — rebuild it as native animation.
Manim for math; Remotion for charts and diagrams.
The ASK→RESULT law: every visual has a traceable prompt.
If Claude can't build it: a REQUEST CARD names what it needs.

beats.narration_text: Here's the prompt. I have a figure showing a precision-recall curve from my classifier paper. Rebuild it as a native Remotion animated chart — terracotta bars, spring animation, with the ASK to RESULT receipt below. Run that prompt with your figure attached, and Claude will produce the animated scene. Episode ten covers watching and revising — that's next.

props.command: I have a precision-recall curve figure. Rebuild it as a native Remotion animated chart — terracotta bars, spring animation — with the ASK→RESULT receipt below.

beats.narration_text: Make It Move. Episode nine of twelve. Next: Watch and Revise.
